title: Uniaxial strain-induced phase transition in the 2D topological semimetal IrTe2

paper_analysis.summary: This paper shows that modest uniaxial tensile strain (~0.1%) stabilizes the long-debated 6×1 charge-ordered ground state of IrTe2, replacing the usual mixed low-temperature phase landscape. Using ARPES, STM, XPS, LEED, and DFT, the authors link strain-induced Ir→Te charge transfer to weakened interlayer Te–Te bonding, reduced interlayer hopping, and a Lifshitz transition that exposes previously inaccessible bulk Dirac-like states.
measurement_types: Density functional theory (DFT) charge density / bonding analysis
Strain gauge calibration / mechanical strain measurement
Magnetic susceptibility and resistivity characterization (sample characterization)

figures.description: High-temperature 1×1 crystal structure of IrTe2 in top and side view.
Low-temperature 6×1 crystal structure showing Ir–Ir dimers and unit cell.
Bulk Brillouin zone of the high-temperature phase with high-symmetry points.
Photograph of the home-built strain device used to apply uniaxial tensile strain.
ARPES band dispersion of unstrained IrTe2 at high temperature along L-A-L.
ARPES band dispersion of unstrained IrTe2 at low temperature showing broadened mixed-phase features.
ARPES band dispersion of strained IrTe2 at low temperature showing sharp bands and 6×1 phase features.
Fermi surface of unstrained high-temperature phase.
Fermi surface of unstrained low-temperature phase with broken threefold symmetry.
Fermi surface of strained low-temperature phase showing directional symmetry breaking and 6×1 periodicity.
Large-scale STM image of unstrained sample showing mixed rotational domains.
Large-scale STM image of strained sample showing a single unidirectional domain.
LEED pattern of unstrained sample.
Atomic-resolution STM image of unstrained sample with multiple 3n+2 periodicities.
LEED pattern of strained sample showing single-domain 6×1 periodicity.
Atomic-resolution STM image of strained sample showing 6×1 periodicity.
Line profile through STM topography showing two inequivalent groups of three atoms in the 6×1 unit cell.
Real-space ARPES intensity map showing millimeter-scale extent of the strained 6×1 phase.
Ir 4f7/2 XPS core-level spectra at 300 K comparing strained and unstrained samples; strained sample shows Ir3+δ+ shoulder.
Ir 4f7/2 XPS core-level spectra at 30 K comparing strained and unstrained samples; strained sample shows enhanced Ir3+δ+ peak.
DFT-calculated electronic density for the high-temperature 1×1 phase.
DFT-calculated electronic density for the 6×1 phase showing increased interlayer charge density.
Calculated relative interlayer bond strengths in the 6×1 phase from COHP analysis compared with the high-temperature phase.
Photon-energy-dependent ARPES kz dispersion comparing strained low-temperature and unstrained high-temperature phases, with tight-binding fits.
ARPES cuts near the A-point showing bulk Dirac-like hyperbolic dispersions in the strained 6×1 phase.
Fermi surface around the A-point highlighting in-plane structure of Dirac and surface states and the cut positions used in Fig. 4c.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include ARPES spectra and Fermi-surface maps (including photon-energy-dependent kz scans and micro-ARPES maps), XPS core-level spectra, STM topography images, LEED diffraction patterns, and sample characterization by resistivity and magnetic susceptibility. The paper also describes direct strain-gauge voltage measurements used to calibrate applied strain.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed/derived plots include STM line cuts, real-space ARPES intensity maps, DFT charge-density isosurfaces, COHP-based bond-strength comparisons, and tight-binding fits to extracted ARPES dispersions/Fermi-surface warping. Several ARPES panels are also presentation-level processed images of intensity versus energy-momentum.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'Data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.' Supplementary information is available online, but no public repository, DOI, accession number, or explicit source-data archive is mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of measurement modality and instrument: ARPES/XPS at Diamond I05 and SLS PEARL, micro-ARPES at University of Fribourg, STM at a low-temperature STM, LEED images, and strain-gauge voltage readout. No file formats, directory structure, or raw-data repository are specified.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted peak ratios from XPS, fitted kz contours and tight-binding parameters from ARPES, STM line profiles, real-space ARPES intensity maps, DFT-derived charge densities, and COHP bond-strength values. These are described in the text and figure captions, but no tabulated dataset is mentioned.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are shown throughout the main figures and supplementary figures are referenced (S1–S7, S3, S4, S5, S6, S7), but the paper does not state that per-figure source data files are deposited or linked.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned. Computational methods are described (VASP, LOBSTER, tight-binding model), but not shared as scripts.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide a public source-data package, raw files, or scripts. Although many figures are based on experimental and calculated data, the text only says data are available on reasonable request, so the dataset is not directly replot-ready from the paper alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper mixes raw experimental images/spectra with derived analyses and model fits. The strongest evidence for figure-level data is in the captions and methods, but there is no explicit statement of 'source data' files or a structured dataset release. Supplementary figures likely contain additional processed plots, but their underlying files are not described.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The methods explicitly describe collecting ARPES, XPS, STM, LEED, strain-gauge, resistivity, and susceptibility measurements, indicating raw experimental data were generated.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: No explicit source-data archive, repository, or per-figure source-data statement is given; only 'Data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.'
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not infer a public source-data package from the presence of many figures. The paper clearly contains raw measurements and derived analyses, but the provided text does not document downloadable raw files, source-data spreadsheets, or scripts.
